Court admonishes plaintiff's counsel as to the representation of cited authorities made to the Court and document signed by attorneys that are inaccurate. Court determines that this is a breach of the duty of candor to the tribunal. Court takes corrective action and orders that counsel for plaintiff, Jessica Ng is to take an Ethics CLE course (one hour course) specifically concerning the duties of candor to the Court and the diligence that is represented in signing a document to the Court, within 4 months. Aaron Darsky, Esq., who signed off on the misrepresentation of authorities to the court, is also ordered to take an Ethics CLE course (one hour course). Certification of completion is to be sent to the Court with a letter cc'ed to opposing counsel. Certification does not need to be filed.

The Court modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS CAMAL EL SHERIFI, DAVID BAKER, LEA CURRY, MOHAMED SADI, MOHAMED ABID, MOUSLIH NEZHA, and CELSO BORGES's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One From Defendant Mercy Housing Management Group, Inc.; Request For Monetary Sanctions (Amended).

Plaintiffs bring a motion to compel further responses to their Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, pursuant to CCP sec. 2030.310. (The court notes that this motion appears to seek relief under both sec. 2030.310(a), motions to compel amended responses to address, e.g., unmeritorious objections, and sec. 2030.320(a), motions to order compliance with a party's representation of what documents it will produce. Because the moving papers made clear from the outset what Plaintiffs sought-further production of documents where they believed Mercy's production was incomplete, along with rulings on objections-the court exercises its discretion to consider Plaintiffs' entitlement to this relief, notwithstanding that their notice of motion did not specify both code sections.) For the reasons below, the court grants the motion in part. [see word minutes for complete details)

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF CAMAL EL SHERIFI, DAVID BAKER, LEA CURRY, MOHAMED SADI, MOHAMED ABID, MOUSLIH NEZHA, AND CELSO BORGES' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One From Defendant Mercy Housing Management Group, Inc.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Plaintiffs' Requests for Production of Documents, Set One from Defendant Mercy Housing Management Group, Inc. is continued to February 28, 2025.

Plaintiff filed this motion on October 24, 2024, and then re-calendared it without changing the substance of the motion. Defendant produced additional documents on October 29 and 30 and on November 4. (Supp. Darsky Decl. 6-7.) Plaintiff admits that defendant supplemented its responses to Nos. 5, 7, 12, 21, 25, 32, 45, 47, and 48 and refused to supplement its responses to Nos. 23, 8, 43, 15, and 16. (Supp. Darsky Decl. 7.) The court orders the parties to continue to meet and confer regarding whether the supplemented responses moot at least a portion of this motion and whether a privilege log should be produced. Plaintiff may file a 5-page supplemental brief and an updated separate statement by February 10. Defendant may file a 5-page supplemental brief and opposing separate statement by February 20. The court notes that the sanctions issue is still live. (See CRC 3.1348(a) [court may award sanctions where discovery is provided after a motion is filed].)

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
